agencies.name: Department of Health and Mental Hygiene
Dept. of Youth and Community Dev.
Dept. of Records and Information Services
Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities / MOPT
Dept. of Social Services / Human Resources Administration

agreements.title: DARB Data Share MOU & BAA
agreements.description: DOF and HRA share Medicaid Surplus Program client data to enable online payment processing for eligible individuals paying excess income to local social services districts, with DOF receiving daily electronic transfers of client identifying information for payment operations.

agreements.title: HASA Pilot Program Data Sharing
agreements.description: DSS/HRA, DOHMH, and CUNY share HASA client data including names, SSNs, report dates, and HIV-related laboratory results to evaluate three pilot housing programs (Permanent Supportive Housing, Transitional Supportive Housing, and Commercial Single-Room Occupancy Housing) aimed at improving housing stability and HIV viral suppression outcomes.

agreements.title: IDNYC Middle School Cards MOU
agreements.description: HRA and DOE establish a pilot program to issue IDNYC Middle School Cards to students ages 10 and up enrolled in participating DOE middle schools. The agreement sets forth the enrollment process, roles, and responsibilities for card issuance.

agreements.title: IDNYC Enrollment Site MOU
agreements.description: DSS/HRA grants DYCD read-only access to its participant tracking system to verify that youth applying for NYC Identification Cards (IDNYC) are enrolled in DYCD's Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) or Work, Learn, and Grow Employment Program (WLGEP).

agreements.title: Test and Trace Robocalls Intra-City Agreement
agreements.description: HRA and DOHMH share Coronavirus-related health guidance and testing resources by using HRA's telecommunications vendors to send automated robocalls with recorded health and safety messages to NYC residents in targeted neighborhoods.

agreements.title: DOPIS Data Sharing Agreement
agreements.description: OMH shares monthly data on individuals proposed for discharge from NYC-area psychiatric centers after five or more years of stay with HRA/DOPIS to enable assessment of discharge eligibility, service provision, and supportive housing placement.

agreements.title: HRA-DOHMH Health Statistics Cooperative Agreement FY20-22
agreements.description: HRA provides funding and oversight for DOHMH to deliver Medicaid application assistance, health insurance education, and managed care support to uninsured and Medicaid-eligible New Yorkers, including targeted outreach and enrollment services for vulnerable populations.

agreements.title: Finance-HRA Cooperative Agreement
agreements.description: The Department of Finance's Sheriff's Division provides law enforcement officers and support personnel to the Human Resources Administration's Office of Child Support Enforcement to serve summons and subpoenas and execute arrest warrants issued by the Family Court in child support cases.

agreements.title: Inter-Agency Data Exchange Agreement (HHS)
agreements.description: Health and human services agencies share client data through HHS-Connect infrastructure to improve service delivery, reduce data duplication, and enable integrated case management across multiple benefit and housing programs.

agreements.title: MyCity Childcare Portal Data Sharing Agreement
agreements.description: OTI, ACS, DHS, DOE, and HRA share childcare-related data to develop a unified online intake form for subsidized childcare services through the MyCity digital portal. Data sources include ACS's childcare information systems, HRA's enterprise data warehouse, and DHS homeless services records.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY - Unlocking Unemployment Interagency Agreement
agreements.description: NYC Opportunity, HRA, and CUNY collaborate to fund and deliver the 'Unlocking Employment' online learning program that helps workforce development providers serve people with criminal records by developing training materials and educational content.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY - HSE Connect Renewal Interagency Agreement
agreements.description: NYC Opportunity and HRA provide funding to CUNY to support HSE Connect, an initiative connecting criminal justice system-involved adults lacking high school credentials to High School Equivalency programming.

agreements.title: HRA - NYC Parks - OCSS Snapshot
agreements.description: HRA/DSS and NYC Parks share access to OCSS's Child Support Snapshot portal system to enable the CBO to access child support information about non-custodial and custodial parents, facilitating employment counseling, financial planning, and other services to improve child support compliance and reduce child poverty.

agreements.title: HRA - DYCD - Cooperative Agreement TANF Funding
agreements.description: HRA provides funding to DYCD to support the Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) for eligible NYC youth aged 14-20. DYCD recruits participants, manages program enrollment, develops work sites, collects payroll records, and issues payments and tax documents.

agreements.title: HRA - MOPT - ERAP DataShare agreement
agreements.description: HRA/DSS shares Emergency Rental Assistance Program (ERAP) data with the Mayor's Office to Protect Tenants to support ERAP implementation, inform outreach strategy, track community-based organizations' assistance efforts, and avoid duplication of assistance to tenants and landlords.

agreements.title: DSS.DOC Data Share MOU July 1, 2021
agreements.description: DOC provides access to its HartIsland burial database to DSS and its contractors to facilitate the transition of Hart Island burial grounds management responsibility from DOC to DSS pursuant to NYC Administrative Code section 21-110.

agreements.title: DSS - Parks - Corrections Hart Island MOU
agreements.description: Department of Correction, Parks and Recreation, and Department of Social Services coordinate operations and jurisdiction over Hart Island burial grounds, with DOC transferring control to Parks on July 1, 2021, while DSS establishes contracts for burial and disinterment services during a transition period.

agreements.title: HRA - COSTCO - IDNYC Benefit Partner MOU. June 7, 2021
agreements.description: HRA and Costco Corporation establish a data sharing arrangement to support the NYC Identification Card Program (IDNYC), whereby Costco provides membership discounts to IDNYC cardholders through Membership Activation Certificates and a dedicated online portal.

agreements.title: ACS - HRA Cash Assistance Data Share MOU Renewal. June 7, 2021
agreements.description: ACS and HRA share data on Mutual Recipients (families receiving CA benefits) to help identify and support families at risk of losing benefits due to non-compliance with employment and child support requirements, and to track effectiveness of interventions.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY MOIA Action NYC Modification Agreement. May 27, 2021
agreements.description: HRA, MOIA, and CUNY partner to administer Action NYC, providing free immigration legal services to immigrants in NYC. This fifth modification increases funding to support COVID-19 emergency relief for immigrant workers and additional program initiatives.

agreements.title: HRA - Brook Homes Mgt, LLC. - Emergency Housing SROs. January 6, 2021
agreements.description: HRA refers eligible persons needing emergency housing to Brook Homes Management LLC, which operates Single Room Occupancy (SRO) facilities to provide emergency shelter and related services to HRA clients.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY - EDGE Modification Coop Agreement. February 11, 2020
agreements.description: HRA and CUNY collaborate to provide paid work study positions to Cash Assistance recipients enrolled in two- or four-year CUNY colleges under the EDGE (Education, Development, Graduate and Empower) Program, supporting workforce preparation and employment.

agreements.title: HRA - DOF - DARB Data Share MOU & BAA. January 28, 2020
agreements.description: DOF and HRA share Medicaid Surplus Program client data to enable eligible individuals to make online payments for excess income to the local social services district using credit cards through DOF's online payment portal.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY - DOHMH Data Sharing HASA Pilot Program. December 18, 2019
agreements.description: DSS/HRA, DOHMH, and CUNY share client data including names, Social Security numbers, report dates, and HIV-related laboratory test results to evaluate HASA pilot housing programs (Permanent Supportive Housing, Transitional Supportive Housing, and Commercial Single-Room Occupancy Housing) for fidelity, effectiveness, and replication potential.

agreements.title: HRA - DOE - IDNYC Middle School Cards. November 20, 2019
agreements.description: HRA and DOE establish an enrollment process for the IDNYC Middle School Card, a uniquely formatted IDNYC card available to students ages 10 and up at participating DOE middle schools, with DOE schools distributing applications and verifying student information through their Automate the Schools system.

agreements.title: HRA - CUNY - Fair Fares Data Transfer Amendment. November 18, 2019
agreements.description: CUNY transfers student contact data to NYC Department of Social Services/Human Resources Administration (HRA) for the Fair Fares Program, enabling HRA to send text messages to eligible students about transit subsidies.

agreements.title: HRA - Brooklyn Events Center LLC - IDNYC Barclays discounts. October 18, 2019
agreements.description: NYC Department of Social Services/Human Resources Administration provides IDNYC cardholders access to discounts at Barclays Center through an agreement with Brooklyn Events Center LLC and Brooklyn Nets LLC.

agreements.title: HRA - DVS Inter-Agency Collaboration and Data Exchange Agreement. July 25, 2019
agreements.description: DVS and HRA collaborate to identify and enroll low-income student veterans in NYC-based colleges and universities into the Fair Fares NYC Program to provide discounted MTA Metrocards for public transportation access.

agreements.title: HRA - DPR Parks Opportunity Program. September 27, 2019
agreements.description: HRA refers Public Assistance participants to DPR for seasonal employment placements through the Parks Opportunity Program (POP) and Job Training Partnership (JTP), with DPR providing up to 1,714 supervised work slots and hiring up to 3,000 seasonal employees annually from HRA referrals.

agreements.title: HRA - Kings County DA's Office Fraud Prosecution. September 24, 2019
agreements.description: HRA/Department of Social Services refers to the Kings County District Attorney's office cases where willful acts designed to interfere with proper administration of public assistance are suspected, and permits disclosure of confidential client identifiable Medicaid data for fraud prosecution purposes without individual consent when required by law.

agreements.title: HRA - DHS Data Sharing MOU - EAM Lead Work Orders. September 13, 2019
agreements.description: HRA and DHS share property address information for apartments in pre-1978 buildings serving families with children to support the LeadFreeNYC plan, enabling DHS to create work orders for lead testing and abatement through its EAM system.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA Finance 17-088C addendum. September 13, 2019
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) shares Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) including Protected Health Information with HRA Special Service-Home Care Services Program (HCSP) for purposes supporting the Requestor's project, research, or study related to administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program or services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA Finance 17-088D addendum. September 13, 2019
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) for purposes supporting the Requestor's project, research, or study that assists in administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program or services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: HRA - DOHMH HealthStat Cooperative agreement. July 1, 2019
agreements.description: The State of New York Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance (OTDA) shares information from its Specialized Fraud and Abuse Reporting System (SFARS) with the NYC Department of Social Services Human Resources Administration (HRA) to strengthen trafficking detection efforts in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP).

agreements.title: HRA OTDA - SFARS Data Sharing. July 26, 2019
agreements.description: HRA provides funding and oversight for DOHMH to deliver Medicaid application assistance, health insurance education, and enrollment services to uninsured New Yorkers, including targeted outreach to children eligible for special health care programs.

agreements.title: NYC Health + Hospitals and Mayor's Office to End Domestic and Gender Based Violence MOU
agreements.description: NYC Mayor's Office to End Domestic and Gender-Based Violence (ENDGBV), Department of Social Services/Human Resources Administration (HRA), and NYC Health + Hospitals provide full-time mental health services at NYC Family Justice Centers.

agreements.title: DOE Early Learn transfer MOU
agreements.description: ACS, DOE, and DSS share child care records and enrollment data to facilitate the transfer of the EarlyLearn contracted care system from ACS to DOE, ensuring continued operations and compliance with federal Head Start requirements.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement MICSA 17-088L. October 24, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OPA 17-088Q. September 7 , 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services for purposes supporting administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OPA 17-088R. September 7 , 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) shares Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) with NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management and improvement of State Medicaid services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: HRA – DOHMH Visiting psychiatric services in shelters. August 7, 2018
agreements.description: DOHMH transfers funds to HRA to enable HRA's Visiting Psychiatric Services to Shelters program to provide psychiatric evaluation and case management services to homeless mentally ill individuals in municipal shelters, and HRA's Visiting Psychiatric Services to NY/NY Housing Sites program to provide emergency mental health evaluation services to place homeless mentally ill individuals into housing.

agreements.title: HRA – DYCD Summer Youth Employment Program. August 3, 2018
agreements.description: HRA provides funding through OTDA to support DYCD's Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) for eligible NYC youth aged 14-20. DYCD recruits participants, manages program enrollment, develops work sites, maintains payroll records, and issues compensation documentation.

agreements.title: HRA – DHS Shelter space. August 1, 2018
agreements.description: HRA provides shelter space at 596 E. 170th Street in the Bronx to DHS for homeless clients with minor children. DHS coordinates with Community Outreach Consulting Firm, Inc. (COCF) to place DHS shelter clients at HRA facilities on a temporary basis to address shelter capacity shortages.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement IREA 17-088O. August 29, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support requests for MCD release and enable administration, monitoring, and improvement of State Medicaid programs and services.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement IREA 17-088P. August 29, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) shares Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) with NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: HRA – CUNY – OCDV Domestic violence symposium. July 31, 2018
agreements.description: NYC Mayor's Office to Combat Domestic Violence, Department of Social Services/Human Resources Administration, and City University of New York collaborate to develop and implement a Domestic Violence and Media Fellowship Program, with CUNY hosting a symposium and recruiting journalists to improve domestic violence reporting quality in NYC media.

agreements.title: HRA – CHS Shelter services for inmates. July 31, 2018
agreements.description: H+H and DHS share information to place Brad H Class Members (sentenced individuals identified as homeless) directly into DHS Mental Health Shelters upon release from Department of Correction custody, pursuant to Brad H Stipulation requirements. H+H sends DHS daily encrypted lists of identified homeless inmates, recently released inmates, and transportation information.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA CAS 17-088A addendum. July 23, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data to New York City Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services for purposes supporting Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement CAS 17-088A. July 20, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support requests for MCD release and enable monitoring, management, and improvement of the State Medicaid program.

agreements.title: HRA – NYCHA Rental Arrears payment assistance e-Service
agreements.description: NYCHA provides HRA caseworkers access to its Rental Arrears Information database to identify and assist NYCHA residents in rent arrears who are eligible for HRA rental payment assistance to prevent eviction.

agreements.title: HRA – CUNY- MOIA Free immigration legal services for NYers
agreements.description: HRA through MOIA provides funding to support CUNY and MOIA in delivering free, safe, and high-quality immigration legal services to immigrants residing or working in New York City through a centralized program offering legal screenings, advice, representation, and referrals.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA Finance 17-088C addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) for purposes supporting Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA Finance 17-088D addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) for purposes supporting Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA OPPM 17-088E addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC HRA Special Services-Home Care Services Program (HCSP) to support administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA OPPM 17-088F addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) and NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) share Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) from the DOH Medicaid Data Warehouse to support HRA's administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA OER 17-088G addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support administration, monitoring, and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA OER 17-088H addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services for project support, research, and study purposes related to Medicaid program administration and service improvement.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement HCSP 17-088K. July 20, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) from the DOH Medicaid Data Warehouse to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OLA 17-088M. July 20, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OLA 17-088N. July 20, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support requests for MCD release and administration of State Medicaid programs.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement MIS 17-088I. July 23, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support requests for MCD release and enable HRA to administer, monitor, and improve state Medicaid program services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement MIS 17-088J. July 23, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data to NYC HRA/Department of Social Services to support requests for Medicaid release and administration of State Medicaid programs.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP DUA CAS 17-088B addendum. July 10, 2018
agreements.description: The New York State Department of Health Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services for purposes supporting Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: HRA - NYCHA HRA Rental Payment Assistance
agreements.description: NYCHA provides HRA with access to its Rental Arrears Information database to facilitate HRA's rental payment assistance program for NYCHA residents in rent arrears, helping prevent evictions.

agreements.title: DOHMH-DFTA Facilitated Enrollment MOU
agreements.description: HRA, DFTA, and DOHMH collaborate to provide outreach, education, and enrollment services for disabled, aged, and blind individuals in the Facilitated Enrollment for the Aged, Blind and Disabled Program, with DOHMH placing facilitators at senior centers and health facilities, DFTA providing dedicated enrollment space, and both agencies coordinating data reporting on applications and enrollments through PHS and NYSOH systems.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement Finance 17-088C. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: NYC HRA/DSS and NYS DOH share Medicaid Confidential Data to support HRA's Special Services-Home Care Services Program administration, monitoring, management and improvement of State Medicaid program and services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement Finance 17-088D. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) from the Medicaid Data Warehouse to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OPPM 17-088E. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) shares Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) with NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support requests for MCD release and to assist in administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services provided to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OPPM 17-088F. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs shares Medicaid Confidential Data with NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services to support requests for MCD release for administration, monitoring, management and improvement of the State Medicaid program and services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OER 17-088G. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs (OHIP) shares Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) with NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support requests for MCD release and enable HRA to administer, monitor, manage and improve State Medicaid program and services.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement OER 17-088H. May 10, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Insurance Programs provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support requests for MCD release and assist in administration, monitoring, management and improvement of State Medicaid programs and services to beneficiaries.

agreements.title: NYSDOH OHIP Data Use agreement CAS 17-088B. March 14, 2018
agreements.description: New York State Department of Health (DOH) provides Medicaid Confidential Data (MCD) to NYC Human Resources Administration/Department of Social Services (HRA/DSS) to support Medicaid program administration, monitoring, management, and improvement of state Medicaid services.

agreements.title: DHS-IDNYC-CARES. IDNYC access to DHS client info. Jan. 23, 2018
agreements.description: HRA's IDNYC Program provides DHS access to NYC Municipal Identification Card applicant information to verify information submitted by DHS clients in the homeless services system.

agreements.title: HRA-HHC. IDNYC participation at Metropolitan Hospital. October 20, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and NYC Health + Hospitals collaborate to provide program space and support services at Metropolitan Hospital for the IDNYC Program, which issues identification cards to NYC residents who lack alternative forms of identification.

agreements.title: HRA-Richmond County DA. Referrals for Welfare Fraud. September 19, 2017
agreements.description: NYC Department of Social Services (HRA) and Richmond County District Attorney's Office share welfare fraud case referrals for prosecution. The DA prosecutes Public Assistance, SNAP, and Medical Assistance fraud cases referred by HRA and accrues revenues from successful prosecutions.

agreements.title: HRA-HHC. Comprehensive Health Assessment Team. September 5, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and HHC establish a clinical assessment partnership in which HHC provides Comprehensive Health Assessment Teams at eleven HASA centers to clinically evaluate persons living with AIDS for appropriate housing placement levels.

agreements.title: HRA -CUNY Information Technology Fellowship agreement. July 1, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and CUNY establish a fellowship program where CUNY provides IT Specialist fellows to work at HRA following IT internships, supporting HRA's information technology operations.

agreements.title: HRA-DOHMH. Medicaid Application assistance. July 1, 2017
agreements.description: HRA provides funding and reports for DOHMH to deliver Medicaid application assistance, health insurance education, and outreach services to uninsured and eligible New Yorkers, with particular focus on children and vulnerable populations.

agreements.title: HRA DYCD. Renewals Mod to IDNYC. June 5, 2017
agreements.description: HRA/Department of Social Services and DYCD facilitate enrollment of Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) participants in the IDNYC Program by sharing participant verification data. IDNYC's Integrity Unit accesses participant information to verify SYEP participant status.

agreements.title: HRA SBS. Amd to SNAP E & T Program. June 2, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and SBS share job training and employment services data for SNAP E&T program participants. HRA submits invoices and reimbursement claims to OTDA with 50/50 federal/city match funding.

agreements.title: HRA- NY County DA. Referrals for Welfare Fraud. May 25, 2017
agreements.description: The NYC Department of Social Services/Human Resources Administration shares welfare fraud cases with the New York County District Attorney's Office for prosecution of Public Assistance, SNAP, and Medical Assistance fraud cases.

agreements.title: HRA OCDV MOCJ. Family Justice centers Coop Agree. May 25, 2017
agreements.description: HRA, MOCJ, and OCDV cooperate to deliver intake, assessment, case management, family law legal services, immigration legal services, and children's services at NYC Family Justice Centers, with HRA seeking reimbursement from New York State for Social Services Block Grant allocations.

agreements.title: HRA-DYCD. Summer Youth Employment Program. March 29, 2017
agreements.description: HRA provides funding to DYCD for the Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP), which recruits and employs eligible youth ages 14-20, with DYCD managing participant enrollment, work sites, payroll records, worker's compensation, and tax documentation.

agreements.title: HRA-DHS-MOO. Homestat Operations Renewal. March 1, 2017
agreements.description: HRA, DHS, and Operations share personally identifiable client information to support HOME-STAT (Homeless Outreach and Mobile Engagement Street Action Team), which identifies homeless individuals and connects them with benefits, services, and housing to transition them to permanent housing.

agreements.title: HRA-DOF. Renewal of IDNYC operations. March 1, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and DOF enable the NYC Municipal Identification Card (IDNYC) Program to operate from four DOF business center locations across the five boroughs, allowing HRA to engage with IDNYC applicants.

agreements.title: DOHMH – DSS. HIV/AIDS Data Share to Link to Medical Care. February 3, 2017
agreements.description: DOHMH, HRA, and DHS share HIV/AIDS client data to improve health outcomes and medical care engagement for low-income New Yorkers with HIV or AIDS residing in DHS shelters or enrolled in HRA's HIV/AIDS Services Administration program.

agreements.title: HRA-HPD-MOCs. Resident Data Share with three-quarter Houses. January 31, 2017
agreements.description: Mayor's Office of Operations, Department of Housing Preservation and Development, and Human Resources Administration share identifiable resident data of individuals living in three-quarter houses to coordinate case management services, temporary housing placement, and permanent housing assistance for relocation efforts.

agreements.title: HRA-DHS – IDNYC Client Shelter and Placement Data Share. January 18, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and DHS share DHS client shelter history and placement information via a password-protected database to verify NYC residency of IDNYC applicants and facilitate the IDNYC card application and enrollment process.

agreements.title: HRA – H+H – Comprehensive Health Assessment Team 4th Renewal. January 4, 2017
agreements.description: HRA and NYC Health and Hospitals Corporation renew their cooperative agreement for Comprehensive Health Assessment Team (CHAT) services, extending the contract period from July 1, 2016 to June 30, 2017 with increased funding of $1,709,434.00.

agreements.title: Exchange of Information Agreement
agreements.description: NYC Department of Finance and New York State Department of Taxation and Finance exchange tax information including taxpayer identity, income, payments, deductions, and audit data to administer New York State Tax Law and NYC Administrative Code.

agreements.title: Programmatic Agreement Regarding the City’s Demolition Program
agreements.description: NYC OMB, NY State Historic Preservation Officer, NYC Department of Housing Preservation and Development, NYC Department of Buildings, NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission, and Shinnecock Indian Nation coordinate on the City's demolition program for unsafe and dangerous buildings, with consultation on historic preservation and cultural resources.

agreements.title: MOU regarding Fair Housing Funds for Fair Housing Related Activities
agreements.description: HPD allocates $107,000 to NYCCHR to support activities furthering NYC's fair housing strategy, including fair housing investigations and related activities.

agreements.title: MOU regarding HPD’s provision of inspection services for HRA’s LINC Rental Assistance Programs
agreements.description: HPD performs LINC Apartment Standards inspections of housing units for HRA's Living in Communities rental assistance program to verify physical standards compliance prior to occupant move-in. HRA funds all costs associated with HPD's inspection services.

agreements.title: MOU between HPD and DSNY regarding the inclusion of recycling coordinator information in HPD’s Property Owner Registration System
agreements.description: HPD and DSNY agree to modify HPD's Property Owners Registration System (PROS) to collect and maintain Recycling Coordinator information from residential building owners and transfer that data to DSNY to support NYC recycling mandates and enforcement.

agreements.title: Agreement relating to preparation and submission by HRA of emergency housing services claims on behalf of HPD
agreements.description: HPD and HRA share hotel and shelter cost data for individuals in HPD's Emergency Housing Services program to enable HRA to determine billing eligibility and submit claims for funding to the NYS Office of Temporary Disability and Assistance.

agreements.title: Agreement relating to inter-agency data exchange
agreements.description: HHS-Connect facilitates data integration and exchange between health and human services agencies to improve client outcomes, reduce data duplication, and promote coordinated service delivery across NYC programs. Participating agencies share data from systems including ACCIS, SCRIE, CTS, TDS, HRA EDW, and related repositories.

agreements.title: Agreement relating to electronic payment services
agreements.description: DOF and HPD share payment processing services and electronic payment data through the Citywide Payments and Receivables Repository (CPRR) to improve incoming City payments, collections, and receivables management by expanding electronic payment channels.

agreements.title: Agreement relating to transitional housing services
agreements.description: HRA and HPD modification agreement extending emergency transitional housing services for HRA clients through the Emergency Vacate Program, with revised payment terms and quarterly billing procedures.

agreements.title: Activities Related to the City's Fair Housing Strategy
agreements.description: HPD and DOHMH share housing inspection data and violation information to identify environmental hazards (lead, mold, window guards, vermin) in buildings under DOHMH investigation and to target health interventions in high-risk communities.

agreements.title: Expansion of Existing Web Services for use by the Department of Health and Mental Hygiene
agreements.description: HPD allocates $99,750 in Community Development Block Grant funds to NYCCHR to support activities related to the City's Fair Housing Strategy.

agreements.title: Agreement between NYPD and NYC Aging re: Elder Justice Services Outreach to Crime Victims Aged 60 or Older in Patrol Borough Manhattan North and the 5th Precinct, with First Renewal
agreements.description: NYC Department for the Aging and NYPD share crime-related data for victims aged 60 and older with complaint reports occurring within Patrol Borough Manhattan North and the 5th Precinct.

agreements.title: Agreement between NYC Public Schools and NYC Aging re: Anti-Ageism Initiative
agreements.description: The Department of Education and Department for the Aging partner to implement the Anti-Ageism Initiative, an educational program incorporating age-inclusive curriculum and afterschool activities in NYC schools, with NYC Aging providing grant funds to DOE for teacher and staff compensation.

agreements.title: Agreement between HRA/DSS and NYC Aging re: Housing Support Program
agreements.description: NYC Department for the Aging and HRA/Department of Social Services share case management services, intake assessments, and referrals to provide housing support services to income-eligible older adults threatened with eviction or foreclosure, as mandated by Local Law 20 of 2023.

agreements.title: Agreement between HRA and NYC Aging re: Public Assistance Rider
agreements.description: HRA and DFTA partner to connect Public Assistance Recipients with employment opportunities through DFTA's contractors. HRA administers the Business Link program to refer PA Recipients to job openings and track hiring compliance with Public Assistance Hiring Commitment requirements.

agreements.title: Agreement between DOF and NYC Aging re: Transfer of the SCRIE Program
agreements.description: DFTA and DOF establish a transition agreement for DOF to assume administration of the Senior Citizens Rent Increase Exemption (SCRIE) program from DFTA, with DFTA continuing to process applications and appeals through September 15, 2009, and providing outreach and support services.

agreements.title: Agreement between OTI and NYC Aging re: Connected Communities
agreements.description: OTI and NYC Department for the Aging collaborate to implement the Connected Communities program, which establishes public computer centers at older adult centers to provide internet access and digital resources to senior citizens and other New Yorkers.

agreements.title: CUNY Career Launch Program Interagency Agreement
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY collaborate to provide three workforce development programs (CUNY Career Launch, CUNY Explorers, and STEM Research Academy) to enrolled CUNY students ages 18-24 for career preparation and skills development.

agreements.title: Agreement between DYCD and CUNY for Family Development and Empowerment Skills for Leaders Credential Programs
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY collaborate to deliver professional development curricula and related services for the Family Development Credential Program and Empowerment Skills for Leaders Credential Program, with DYCD providing funding to CUNY for fiscal years 2024-2026.

agreements.title: CUNY Career Launch Program MOU and Amendment
agreements.description: DYCD provides funding to CUNY for workforce development programs serving youth ages 18-24, including the Career Launch Program, CUNY Explorers, and STEM Research Academy for enrolled CUNY students.

agreements.title: Memorandum of Understanding between DYCD and DOHMH NYC Teens Connection for Adolescent Sexual Health Education and Services
agreements.description: DYCD and NYC Teens Connection (NYCTC) collaborate to implement evidence-based adolescent sexual health education programs and clinical linkages to reproductive health services for youth ages 14-24 in DYCD workforce programs, with support from DOHMH and Fund for Public Health in New York City.

agreements.title: Interagency Agreement between DYCD CUNY FDC and ESL Credential Program Agreement
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY on behalf of the JFK Jr. Institute for Worker Education share curriculum delivery and educational services for the Family Development Credential Program and Empowerment Skills for Leaders Credential Program, with DYCD providing additional funding of $35,000 to increase student enrollment through June 30, 2026.

agreements.title: Agreement between DYCD and The City University of New York for the Work Learn and Grow Career Ready Program
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY's Kingsborough Community College collaborate to provide college and career exploration programming and CUNY academic credit to participants in DYCD's Work, Learn, and Grow Employment Program. CUNY provides College Now classes and workshops to 7,000 WLG program participants during the 2025-2026 academic year.

agreements.title: Agreement between DYCD and DOE granting access to DOE swimming pools and other spaces for the purposes of the Program
agreements.description: DYCD and DOE collaborate to implement the Making Waves Program and Lifeguard Training Program, which provides free water safety and swim instruction to youth ages 6-18 in low-income neighborhoods. DOE provides access to swimming pools and related facilities at six locations, while DYCD reimburses DOE for extended use permits and facility costs.

agreements.title: Interagency Agreement between DYCD and CUNY for Change Initiative Summer Institute
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY (on behalf of the CUNY School of Professional Studies' Intergenerational Change Initiative) collaborate on a youth research project where 16-24 year olds serve as co-researchers to collect and analyze data on available resources and care gaps in key Bronx neighborhoods, supporting DYCD's efforts to establish care continuums for youth at risk of juvenile justice involvement.

agreements.title: Interagency Agreement with CUNY to establish the Office of Neighborhood Safety Youth Leadership Council with Second Renewal
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY collaborate on implementation and administration of the Office of Neighborhood Safety Youth Leadership Council, a program to build capacity among young people in MAP communities to plan and implement neighborhood safety initiatives.

agreements.title: Agreement between DYCD and CUNY for the DYCD Scholars Program
agreements.description: DYCD and CUNY (on behalf of the John F. Kennedy Jr. Institute for Worker Education) share resources and provide administrative services to support the DYCD Scholars Program, a youth studies certification program for youth work professionals employed by DYCD-contracted afterschool and youth development agencies.

agreements.title: Renewal of the Intra-Agency Agreement between DYCD and ACS for the Advance and Earn Plus Program

agreements.title: Memorandum of Understanding between DYCD and ACS for the Provision of Cure Violence Services to Youth in or receiving ACS Care
agreements.description: ACS and DYCD share data and coordinate provision of Cure Violence Services, which use evidence-informed approaches to identify and engage individuals involved in gun violence and deploy interventions to curb that behavior.

agreements.title: Memorandum of Understanding between DYCD and HRA for Public Assistance Hiring Commitment Rider
agreements.description: HRA and DYCD partner to connect public assistance recipients with employment opportunities through DYCD's contractors under the Business Link program, with HRA administering the program and DYCD enforcing hiring requirements and assessing compliance.

agreements.title: This MOU supports DCAS' and NYCTA's collaborative efforts to reduce the number of employees appointed provisionally to competitive class titles in the classified service and the length of time of such provisional appointments.
agreements.description: DCAS and NYCTA coordinate on the development, administration, and scoring of civil service examinations for classified competitive class titles unique to NYCTA, with the goal of reducing provisional appointments and their duration.

agreements.title: Amendment to the Agreement for TBTA to take on greater responsibilities to develop, administer and score certain civil service exams.
agreements.description: DCAS and TBTA coordinate civil service examination administration and fee collection for TBTA-specific competitive class titles. TBTA may delegate administrative responsibilities to NYCTA, which would then accept filing fees and process examinations on behalf of TBTA.

agreements.title: Agreement for TBTA to take on greater responsibilities to develop, administer and score certain civil service exams.
agreements.description: DCAS and TBTA coordinate on the development, administration, validation and scoring of civil service examinations for classified titles unique to TBTA, and TBTA assumes greater responsibility for administering civil service examinations and advancement criteria for TBTA-specific positions.

agreements.title: Agreement for DCAS to provide transitional jobs to cash assistance recipients on parole.
agreements.description: HRA and DCAS collaborate to provide transitional subsidized employment through DCAS facilities to Cash Assistance recipients on parole, utilizing TANF funding from the Transitional Jobs 2nd (TJ2) program managed through a State Division of Parole agreement.

agreements.title: Agreement for NYC Transit to take on greater responsibilities to develop, administer, and score certain civil service exams.
agreements.description: DCAS and NYCTA establish a shared responsibility for developing, administering, and scoring civil service examinations for classified competitive class titles unique to NYCTA, with NYCTA taking greater responsibility for examination administration while complying with DCAS protocols and procedures.

agreements.title: Agreement to use 80 Centre Street to assist NYC building owners in complying with Local Law 84/2009 (benchmarking compliance).
agreements.description: City University of New York (CUNY) and Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS) establish a Benchmarking Service Bureau to provide free public assistance regarding Energy Star Portfolio Manager compliance and energy/water benchmarking under Local Law 84/2009. CUNY students staff the bureau, which operates at 80 Centre Street to help building owners establish ESPM accounts and answer benchmarking questions.

agreements.title: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
agreements.description: Seven NYC agencies establish a Memorandum of Understanding to comply with HIPAA requirements for handling Protected Health Information. DOHMH, FDNY, HRA, and OLR are Covered Entities; DOITT, DORIS, and Law Dept. are Business Associates that create, receive, maintain, transmit, access, use, and/or disclose Protected Health Information on behalf of the Covered Entities.

agreements.title: Business Associate Memorandum of Understanding by and between the New York City Law Department and the New York City Health and Hospitals Corporation
agreements.description: NYC Law Department and NYC Health and Hospitals Corporation share protected health information (PHI) and electronic protected health information (ePHI) to enable Business Associate to provide services under their Operating Agreement while maintaining HIPAA compliance and security standards.

agreements.title: Memorandum of Understanding Pursuant to Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act by and among Covered Entity and Business Associate Agencies
agreements.description: Seven NYC agencies (DOHMH, FDNY, HRA, OLR, DOITT, DORIS, and Law Dept.) establish a Memorandum of Understanding to ensure compliance with HIPAA regulations for the safeguarding of protected health information shared among covered entities and business associates in carrying out their respective functions.

agreements.title: Form 1040 Modernized E-File Fed/State Electronic Filing Program
agreements.description: The Department of Health & Mental Hygiene (DOHMH) and the Sheriff's Department collaborate on the removal of subjects of Assisted Outpatient Treatment (AOT) petitions and orders to hospital emergency rooms under Mental Hygiene Law provisions.

agreements.title: Cooperative Agreement
July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021
agreements.description: DOHMH and DOITT collaborate on the COVID-19 Supplemental Round 3-Vaccination Program Plan funded by CDC grant. DOITT provides technology infrastructure including toll-free calling service, data plans, broadband connectivity, internet at dispensing sites, and commercial applications for web mapping, surveys, dashboards, and reporting analysis.

agreements.description: ACS, DHS, and HRA share information about families with children applying for or residing in shelters to enhance service coordination, determine shelter eligibility, improve service planning, and track status changes from application through shelter exit.

agreements.title: August 29, 2012 re Bikeshare Locations
agreements.description: DEP, DOT, and DPR coordinate on the design, installation, and maintenance of green infrastructure stormwater systems in NYC right-of-way locations to reduce combined sewer overflows and meet water quality standards under the State Consent Order.

agreements.description: CUNY and DOB collaborate to streamline NYC solar installation permitting processes by deploying CUNY-funded ombudsmen to educate the solar community on DOB rules and regulations, facilitate inter-agency coordination, and develop a one-stop-shop permitting tracking system.

agreements.title: February 27, 2014 Re: Expansion of Selected DOB violations to be adjudicated by Mail, Internet or Telephone

July 1, 2021 re: Memorandum of Understanding between the Department of Correction and the Department of Citywide Administrative Services, relating to the transfer of Rikers Island parcels
agreements.description: ACS and DOC share information about incarcerated youth ages 16-21 in DOC custody who are in foster care or eligible for foster care services. The agencies coordinate to maintain continuity of ACS-contracted foster care services, facilitate visits, and support transitions from DOC custody to foster care or independent living.

Partnership between NYS DMV and NYC TLC
agreements.description: TLC shares taxicab operator/driver licensing data and paratransit vehicle owner information with HRA/IREA to detect fraud and abuse in public assistance programs including Medicaid, cash assistance, and food stamps, and to verify driver licensing in paratransit billing audits.

agreements.description: DOHMH and DCA coordinate licensing and permitting operations through the Citywide Licensing Center, with DCA handling administrative tasks including application review, fee processing, and customer service for DOHMH licenses and permits.
